MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANTS VERNON LOUCKS and CHARLES LOUCKS's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetay Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,440.00.
It appears from the papers that several of the issues raised in this motion have already been resolved. Counsel are to meet and confer and then to file with the Court a joint statement expressly identifying any issues that have not been resolved. The motion can then be re-noticed for another hearing date, if necessary. An issue raised in the opposition and reply briefs is whether counsel are required to produce documents in their possession that belong to another client. Defendants cite no authority for such a notion, and a third-party subpoena would appear to be the proper method for seeking such documents. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS VERNON R. LOUCKS, JR., and, CHARLES LOUCKS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Kevin Gilbert, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415) 8370456 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Steven J. Serratore, Esq. of Serratore Law (626) 577-4900 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first, second, third, and sixth causes of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for alter ego liability and fifth cause of action for declaratory relief. Alter ego is a theory of liability, not an actual cause of action, and these claims should be pled in the general charging allegation. Declaratory relief cause of action is duplicative of alter ego claim and is not a separate cause of action. This ruling is without prejudice to re-making the motion at a future time, if needed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
